profile.behavior: Active bark forager that moves over trunks, limbs, and weed stems, bracing with stiff tail feathers; often excavates or probes for insects and flies in an undulating, rising-and-falling pattern.
field_marks: Small, compact woodpecker with a blocky head and a notably short, straight chisel-like bill.
Bold black-and-white head stripes frame a dark eye.
Broad white stripe runs down the center of the black back.
Black wings are conspicuously checkered and spotted white.
White outer tail feathers usually show small black spots; males have a small red patch on the nape, while females lack it.
profile.habitat: Open deciduous woodland and wooded edges; also uses orchards, parks, gardens, and other tree-rich areas, especially where dead limbs or trunks provide foraging and nest-cavity sites.

sources.title: Cornell Lab of Ornithology — Downy Woodpecker Identification
Cornell Lab of Ornithology — Downy Woodpecker Life History
Missouri Department of Conservation — Downy Woodpecker Field Guide

findings: Independent verification confirms the species, current scientific name, family, all three measurements, short chisel-like bill, striped head, white back stripe, spotted wings, black-spotted white outer tail feathers, and male red nape.

The illustration agrees closely with the reference photos: compact body, short bill, dark eye, male nape patch, checkered wing, stiff pointed tail, and zygodactyl feet are all plausibly rendered. One wing is visible in profile; the second is naturally occluded. The bird otherwise has one head, one beak, two legs, and one tail.
All visible plate text is factually supported. No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.
No material species, anatomy, or text errors found. Minor stylization in feather/leg detail does not compromise identification or scientific readability.
